task: I’ve got to roll out a small process change to my team and I want the message to be clear without turning into a big speech. Can you help me draft something I could send in a quick note, plus a shorter version I could use if I need to say it out loud?

prefs.explanation: He is driven by results and status, so he likely prefers help that gets to the point quickly. A softened or overly diplomatic style would probably feel inefficient to him. He would respond better to direct wording that makes the action or issue immediately clear.

prefs.short: Prefers concise answers over long explanations
prefs.explanation: His work style is efficiency-focused, and he likely values responses that save time rather than unpack everything. Long-winded help would feel like drag unless the extra detail clearly affects the outcome. He probably wants the shortest answer that still lets him act decisively.

prefs.short: Likes responses in clean bullet points or checklists
prefs.explanation: He seems to prefer information that can be scanned and used fast, especially in a managerial setting. A structured format fits his control-oriented style and reduces ambiguity. He would likely dislike rambling prose when a simple checklist would do.

prefs.short: Can tolerate firm pushback if it protects results
prefs.explanation: He is not looking for emotional cushioning, so a direct warning about risk would likely be acceptable if it is practical. Because he has already been burned by compliance problems, he may respect pushback that prevents mistakes. He would still resist pushback that feels like moralizing or slows execution without clear benefit.

prefs.short: Prefers plain English over fancy wording
prefs.explanation: He seems likely to value clear, practical language that reads like business communication rather than polished rhetoric. Jargon is fine only if it is useful and common in the setting. Overly formal or literary phrasing would probably feel unnecessary to him.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean, direct version you can send, plus a shorter spoken version.

**Quick note:**
Team — small process change starting now: [insert change]. Please make sure you’re following the new step every time so we stay aligned and keep things moving cleanly. If anything is unclear, flag it early.

**Short spoken version:**
Quick update: [insert change] is now in place. Please follow it every time and flag any issues early.

If you want, I can also make it sound more **managerial/direct**, or tailor it to the exact process change.

per_pref.reason: Very blunt and no-nonsense: "small process change starting now" and "Please make sure you’re following the new step every time" get straight to the action.
Concise throughout. It gives a short note and an even shorter spoken version, with no extra explanation.
Uses clean, scannable bullets with clear labels: "Quick note:" and "Short spoken version:".
Includes firm pushback-style guidance without cushioning: "flag it early" and "follow it every time". It’s practical, not moralizing.
Plain English throughout. Phrases like "small process change," "follow it every time," and "flag it early" are simple and practical.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a firmer, more natural version for a banking team.

**Email/text version:**  
Team — starting now, we’re doing **[new process]** every time. This is the standard going forward, so please follow it on every transaction and don’t skip steps. If you run into an issue, raise it right away so we can keep the floor moving and stay consistent.

**Quick huddle version:**  
Quick update: **[new process]** is now the process, and I need everyone following it every time. No skipping steps. If something doesn’t work, flag it straight away.

If you want, I can make it even more like a real branch manager talking to tellers and CSRs.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and blunt: "starting now," "this is the standard going forward," "don’t skip steps," "No skipping steps."
Concise and to the point; gives two short versions without extra explanation.
Uses clean structure with clear labels: "Email/text version" and "Quick huddle version," plus short paragraphs.
Includes firm pushback on noncompliance: "don’t skip steps" and "No skipping steps," while framing it as practical: "keep the floor moving and stay consistent."
Plain English throughout; phrases like "raise it right away" and "flag it straight away" are simple and practical, not fancy.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more branch-floor version.

**Email/text:**  
Team — effective immediately, **[new process]** is the required workflow on every transaction. No exceptions and no skipped steps. We’re handling it this way going forward, so make sure it’s done the same way every time. If you run into a problem, escalate it right away.

**Quick huddle:**  
Quick update — **[new process]** is the process now. Everyone follows it, every time. No shortcuts. If there’s a problem, say it right away.

If you want, I can also make it sound a little more **bank-ops specific** — like month-end, file cleanup, approvals, or exceptions.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and blunt: "effective immediately," "No exceptions and no skipped steps," "No shortcuts." Gets straight to the action.
Concise and efficient. It gives a short email/text and an even shorter spoken version without extra explanation.
Uses clean, scannable formatting with labeled sections: "Email/text:" and "Quick huddle:" plus short paragraphs.
Includes firm pushback that protects results, e.g. "No exceptions" and "If you run into a problem, escalate it right away." It is practical rather than moralizing.
Plain English throughout: "required workflow," "No shortcuts," "say it right away." No fancy or literary wording.
